promptRules: Create full-color artwork suitable for direct-to-garment apparel printing.
Detailed illustrations, photo-like texture, and many colors are acceptable.
Do not request all-over coverage, seam-to-seam wrapping, embroidery texture, foil, puff ink, or metallic effects.
Keep important artwork inside the configured print area and away from garment seams, cuffs, collars, and edges.
Create standalone print artwork only, not an ecommerce product photo or garment mockup.

DTFlex.summary: Premium direct-to-film style printing with sharp detail, vivid color, and clean edges.
promptRules: Create sharp, high-contrast print artwork with clean edges and durable direct-to-film style output.
Fine art, vivid color, and intricate graphic detail are acceptable.
Avoid all-over coverage, seam wrapping, distressed transparency halos, embroidery texture, foil, puff ink, or metallic effects.
Keep the design as a contained print graphic inside the configured placement area.
Create standalone print artwork only, not an ecommerce product photo or garment mockup.

Embroidery.summary: Thread embroidery on garments and accessories.
promptRules: Create a simple embroidery-ready mark with bold shapes, thick lines, and limited detail.
Use flat color regions and avoid gradients, photographic detail, tiny text, thin strokes, halftones, and distressed texture.
Assume the final result is stitched thread, so keep the design compact and readable at small size.
Create standalone embroidery artwork only, not an ecommerce product photo or garment mockup.

Sublimation.summary: Full-product printing on ready-made products such as mugs, phone cases, and socks.
promptRules: Create a repeatable or large-scale full-product design.
Avoid localized chest-print assumptions unless the selected base product explicitly supports that placement.
Keep critical text and faces away from trim, folds, and wrap edges.
Create standalone print artwork only, not an ecommerce product photo or garment mockup.

All-Over Cotton.summary: Printful All-over cotton / direct-to-fabric production for cotton cut-and-sew panels.
promptRules: Design the whole garment first: front body, back body, left sleeve, right sleeve, label panel, and inside label must feel like one apparel system.
Use cotton-appropriate soft or muted colors, matte texture, and water-based pigment ink assumptions.
Use full-panel backgrounds and intentional sleeves; do not paste one square poster onto the garment.
Keep important text, small marks, and logos away from seams, collars, cuffs, underarms, and panel edges.
Use large, low-detail shapes, pinstripes, waves, stripes, and restrained typography that can survive cut-and-sew registration variance.
Exact readable slogans and microcopy are rendered by local deterministic composition, not by the image model.
Create a garment design concept or panel artwork, not a stock ecommerce product photo.

All-Over Synthetic.summary: Cut-and-sew synthetic all-over printing with sharp detail and vibrant color.
promptRules: Create a vibrant all-over synthetic apparel pattern with sharp contrast and saturated color.
Design for cut-and-sew placement across panels, sleeves, hems, and seams.
Avoid single small chest-print layouts unless the selected base product explicitly supports them.
Keep important text away from seams and panel edges.
Create standalone print artwork only, not an ecommerce product photo or garment mockup.
Create a knitwear-ready jacquard pattern using no more than four colors.
Use pixel-like hard edges and avoid gradients, photos, fine text, and transparent effects.
Design as fabric-integrated pattern artwork, not a printed ink layer.
Create standalone knit pattern artwork only, not an ecommerce product photo or garment mockup.
